extra.reasoning: The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) holds on every reachable execution. Analysis: (1) sent is set to true ONLY by [t_send_allowed], whose guard requires not opted_out, so at the moment sent becomes true, opted_out is false. (2) opted_out is set to true ONLY by [t_optout], which in the same atomic update also sets sent := false. (3) There is no transition that ever sets opted_out back to false, so opt-out is permanent (a monotone latch). (4) After opted_out is true, every event keeps sent false: send_attempt matches [t_send_blocked] (sent := false), and all key_0/key_1/key_2 transitions (adv_* and rst_*) explicitly set sent := false; [t_send_allowed] can never fire because its guard not opted_out is permanently false. Therefore there is no reachable state with opted_out = true and sent = true. The combination-lock stage machinery (key_2 advances stage 0->1, key_0 advances 1->2, key_1 advances 2->3, key_0 advances 3->4, with mismatched keys resetting to stage 0) is a distractor: none of the stage transitions can set sent := true, and reaching stage 4 grants no send capability. The bound of 7 events is irrelevant since the invariant is established structurally for all reachable states.
